'''Mafoa'aeata "Ata" Hingano''' (born 11 March 1997) is a [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sh|so}} or {{rlp|hk}} for the [[York Knights]] in the [[Super League]].

He has played for the [[New Zealand Warriors]] and the [[Canberra Raiders]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]]. Hingano also played for the [[Mackay Cutters]] in the [[Queensland Cup]], trained with the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] in the [[National Rugby League]], and played for [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Hingano was born in [[Auckland]], New Zealand, and is of [[Tonga]]n descent.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/tonga-excited-by-future-with-andrew-johns/tabid/10874/newsid/95037/default.aspx|title=Tonga excited by future with Andrew Johns|publisher=NRL.com|date=|access-date=2016-08-21}}</ref> He attended [[Pakuranga College]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.times.co.nz/sports-news/ata-boy-hingano-joins-warriors-reserves.html|title=Ata boy. Hingano joins Warriors reserves - Times Live|publisher=Times.co.nz|date=2016-04-27|access-date=2016-08-21|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160822030148/http://www.times.co.nz/sports-news/ata-boy-hingano-joins-warriors-reserves.html|archive-date=22 August 2016|url-status=dead}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Early career===
He played his junior rugby league for the [[Pakuranga Jaguars]], before being signed by the [[New Zealand Warriors]].

From 2014 to 2016, Hingano played for the New Zealand Warriors' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://nycdatabase.org/players/h/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150706075110/http://nycdatabase.org/players/h/|url-status=usurped|archive-date=6 July 2015|title=H|publisher=Nyc Database|date=|access-date=2016-08-21}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|author=Tim Costello|url=http://leagueunlimited.com/news/leagueunlimited-nyc-teams-2016-round-25/|title=LeagueUnlimited Holden Cup U20s NYC Teams - 2016 Round 24|publisher=League Unlimited|date=2016-08-16|access-date=2016-08-21}}</ref> On 12 December 2014, he re-signed with the Warriors on a 2-year contract until the end of 2016.<ref>{{cite web|author=Warriors.co.nz|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/new-contracts-for-fusitua-and-hingano-16035/#thhOgpiAAFB7MoKJ.97|title=New contracts for Fusitu'a and Hingano|publisher=Zero Tackle|date=2014-12-12|access-date=2016-08-21}}</ref> In 2015 he also spent some time with the Warriors' [[NSW Cup]] team.

===2016===
In 2016, Hingano played for the Warriors at the [[2016 Auckland Nines]].<ref>{{cite web| url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=11581524| title=Shaun Johnson, Tuivasa-Sheck to play at NRL Nines| publisher=nzherald.co.nz| date=29 January 2016| access-date=29 January 2016}}</ref> He also spent some time with the Warriors' Under-20's and [[Intrust Super Premiership NSW]] teams.<ref>{{cite web|author=NRL|url=http://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2015/06/23/vb_nsw_cup_teams_rd_.html|title=VB NSW Cup Teams Rd 16|publisher=NSWRL|date=|access-date=2016-08-21}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|author=NRL|url=http://www.warriors.kiwi/news/2016/06/28/isp_and_nyc_teams_na.html|title=ISP and NYC teams named for 17th round - Warriors|publisher=Warriors.kiwi|date=|access-date=2016-08-21}}</ref>

On 7 May, Hingano played at halfback for the [[Junior Kiwis]] against the [[Junior Kangaroos]], scoring two tries in the Kiwis' 20–34 loss.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/representative-round-team-lists/tabid/10874/newsid/95512/default.aspx|title=Updated: Representative Round team lists|publisher=NRL.com|access-date=2016-05-24|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160604182259/http://www.nrl.com/representative-round-team-lists/tabid/10874/newsid/95512/default.aspx|archive-date=4 June 2016|url-status=dead|df=dmy-all}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://matchcentre.nrl.com/match/2016/133/20161330120/?cid=NRL_Draw_Widget#match-report|title=NRL Match Centre 2016|publisher=Matchcentre.nrl.com|date=|access-date=2016-08-21}}</ref> In round 24 of the [[2016 NRL season]], he made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for the Warriors against the [[North Queensland Cowboys]], coming on at five eighth from the interchange bench.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/updated-team-lists-cowboys-v-warriors/tabid/10874/newsid/100451/default.aspx|title=Updated team lists: Cowboys v Warriors|publisher=NRL.com|date=2016-02-28|access-date=2016-08-21}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|author=NRL Digital Media|url=http://www.warriors.kiwi/news/2016/08/20/nrl_late_mail_v_cowb.af_news.html|title=NRL LATE MAIL | v Cowboys - Warriors|publisher=Warriors.kiwi|date=|access-date=2016-08-21|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160914162627/http://www.warriors.kiwi/news/2016/08/20/nrl_late_mail_v_cowb.af_news.html|archive-date=14 September 2016|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===2017===
Hingano signed a three-year contract with the Warriors, until the end of the 2020 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.warriors.kiwi/news/2017/06/12/_signings_new_deals_.html|title=[Signings] New deals for six|first=NRL Digital|last=Media|publisher=|access-date=15 June 2017}}</ref>

At the end of the year, he was selected to play for [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] in the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]].  He was the starting halfback in every match of the tournament including their semi-final loss to [[England national rugby league team|England]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/mark-geyer-says-jason-taumalolo-has-changed-the-world-cup-forever/news-story/0708c55290b3d8d36727d7a453431a31 |title=Mark Geyer says Jason Taumalolo has changed the World Cup forever |publisher=news.com.au |date=27 November 2017 |access-date=1 June 2022}}</ref>

===2018===
On 28 February 2018, Hingano was released by the [[New Zealand Warriors]] to join the [[Canberra Raiders]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2018/02/28/warriors-release-ata-hingano/ |title=Warriors Release Ata Hingano |publisher=NSW Rugby League |date=28 February 2018 |access-date=1 June 2022}}</ref>

He also retained his place in the [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] squad as halfback for their historic first Test match against the [[Australian Kangaroos]] on October 20, but left the field with a dislocated shoulder early in the match.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.raiders.com.au/news/2018/09/19/mate-maa-tonga-names-squad-for-test-with-kangaroos/ |title=Mate Ma'a Tonga Names Squad For Test With Kangaroos |publisher=Raiders |date=19 September 2018 |access-date=1 June 2022}}</ref>

===2019===
Hingano made no appearances for Canberra in the [[2019 NRL season]]. Hingano instead played for Canberra's feeder club [[Mount Pritchard Mounties|Mounties]] in the [[Canterbury Cup NSW]] competition. Hingano played for Mounties in their elimination final loss against [[Newtown Jets|Newtown]] at [[Campbelltown Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/draw/nsw-cup/2019/finals-week-1/mounties-v-jets/|title=Finals week 1|website=NSWRL}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/players/nsw-cup/mounties/ata-hingano/|title=Player profile|website=NSWRL}}</ref>

On 13 October, it was announced that Hingano had been released by Canberra.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-contracts-squad-lists-2020-signings-esan-marsters-tigers-cowboys-market-watch-scout/news-story/4189eefd9056632397ffd8122cee8557/|title=Every club's squad, new deals and market moves for 2020 season|website=Fox Sports|date=14 October 2019}}</ref> However, it was later revealed that Hingano would remain with the club as part of the development squad in 2020,<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.raiders.com.au/news/2019/11/21/raiders-pre-season-training-squad-semi-valemi-added-to-nrl-squad/|title=Raiders Pre-Season training squad: Semi Valemei added to NRL squad|website=raiders.com.au}}</ref> before his release was once again confirmed two weeks later.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.raiders.com.au/news/2019/12/10/matt-frawley-returns-to-raiders/|title=Matt Frawley returns to Raiders|website=raiders.com.au|date=10 December 2019}}</ref>

On 16 December, he signed with the [[Mackay Cutters]] for the 2020 [[Queensland Cup]] season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2019/12/16/cutters-sign-experienced-international-hingano/|title = Cutters sign experienced international Hingano|date = 16 December 2019}}</ref>

===2020===
Hingano played just one game for the Cutters in [[2020 Queensland Cup|2020]] before the season was canceled due to the [[COVID-19 pandemic]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2020/03/14/norths-devils-pip-mackay-cutters-in-a-thriller/|title=Norths Devils pip Mackay Cutters in a thriller|date=14 March 2020|website=QRL}}</ref> In November, Hingano joined the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] for pre-season training on a train-and-trial deal ahead of the [[2021 NRL season]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.themorningbulletin.com.au/news/cutters-coach-happy-for-cowboys-to-pluck-playmaker/4147320/|title=Cutters coach happy for Cowboys to pluck playmaker from ISC|date=26 November 2020|website=The Morning Bulletin}}</ref>

===2021===
On 15 June 2021 it was reported that he had signed for [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=15 Jun 2021|accessdate=16 Jun 2021|title=Ata Hingano: Struggling Salford sign Tonga half-back until end of 2021 season|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57492539}}</ref>
On 19 November 2021 it was announced that Hingano had signed for [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] in the [[RFL Championship|Betfred Championship]] for the 2022 season.<ref>{{Cite web|last=Darbyshire|first=Drew|date=2021-11-19|title=Leigh snap up Tonga star Ata Hingano from Salford|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leigh-snap-up-tonga-star-ata-hingano-from-salford/|access-date=2021-11-19|website=LoveRugbyLeague|language=en-GB}}</ref>

===2022===
On 10 July, Hingano scored two tries for Leigh in a 66–0 victory over [[Workington Town]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.leighjournal.co.uk/news/20269245.nene-macdonald-hat-trick-leighs-big-win-workington/|title=Nene Macdonald hat-trick in Leigh's big win at Workington|website=www.leighjournal.co.uk}}</ref>
In July, Hingano joined RFL Championship side [[York Knights|York RLFC]] on loan for the remainder of the season. This was later upgraded into a three-year permanent deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ata-hingano-commits-york-city-knights/|title=Tonga ace Ata Hingano commits his long-term future to York|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>

===2025===
On 7 June, Hingano played in York's 1895 Cup final victory over [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]] in a season where York earned promotion to [[English Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cm26dp2l3vvo |title=York pip Featherstone in extra time to win 1895 Cup |work=BBC Sport |date=7 June 2025 |access-date=8 June 2025}}</ref>

===2026===
Hingano started as stand off and starred for [[York City Knights|York RLFC]] in the first match of the [[English Super League]] season as they defeated the 2025 championship winners [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] 19–18 with a 30-metre left footed drop goal,it was the first drop goal of his career.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match-reports/hull-kr-shocked-york-knights-10812311.amp/|title=Hull KR shocked by York Knights as Rovers lick wounds before Brisbane challenge|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk}}</ref>
